content.full_text:
Flrn Pollucar Pucucps CouvussroN
P.O. Box 807 . 428 J Street . Sacramento. CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) 322-0886
May 25,2000
Diane Guyon
Office of the Registrar
P.O. Box 678001
Placerville, CA 95 667-800 1
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-00-105
Dear Ms. Guyon:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").r Since you are not requesting advice on the duties of a specific
public official, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation
r8329(c).'z
QUESTION
The County of El Dorado is holding a mail-in ballot election on August 8, 2000. The
election is for the purpose of establishing an additional parcel fee assessment in the Georgetown
Fire Protection District. The mail-in ballots will be sent to all registered voters within the fire
protection district. Since the first and second preelection statements include the reporting period
covered by the semi-annual statement due July 3l,zOOO,may committees formed to support or
oppose this measure combine their semi-annual statement with their second preelection
statement?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Any committee formed prior to June 30, 2000, for the pulpose of supporting or
opposing your August 8, 2000 measure, may combine the semi-annual statement with the second
preelection statement. This combined statement must be filed no later than July 27,2000, the
deadline for the second preelection statement.
rGovernment Code Section 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18995,
of the California Code of Regulations.
2lnformal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Govemment Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)


File No. I-00-105
Page2
ANALYSIS
Committees formed to support or oppose a ballot measure are required to file trvo
campaign statements disclosing conhibutions received and expendihues made in connection with
that election. These preelection campaign statements are generally filed 45 days before the
election and again 12 days before the election. (Section 84200.8.) Additionally, ballot meiuure
committees must file quarterly statements and semi-annual statements. Semi-annual statements
are filed January 31 and July 31, respectively. (Section 84200.)
The County of El Dorado is holding an election on August 8, 2000. The first preelection
statement filed in connection with this election is June 29,2000, and covers the reporting period
of January 1 through lvne 24. The second preelection statement is due luly 27,2000, and covers
the reporting period of June 25 through Jluly 22,2000. Any ballot measure committee formed
prior to June 30, 2000, would be required to file a semi-annual statement by July 31 covering the
period June 25 through June 30.
There is a mechanism in the Act which permits the combining of statements when an
overlapping reporting period occurs or the filing of the semi-annual statement would be an
additional, unnecessary filing to the two preelection statements. (Section 84205.) Such is the
case with the County of El Dorado's August 8, 2000, election. It appears that combining the
seoond preelection statement with the semi-annual statement would be appropriate for your
election.
AccordinglY, My committee which came into existence prior to June 30, 2000, formed to
support or oppose the August 8, 2000, mail-in ballot measure may combine its semi-arurual
statement with its second preelection statement. The date for filing this combined statement is
July 27,2000, the date the second preelection statement is due.
I trust this answers your question. If I may be of any further assistance, please do not
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General
Jeanette E. Turvill
Political Reform Consultant
KG/rET/jt
Technical Assistance Division
